## 1. Lincoln University (NZ)

**Degree:** Level 9 - Master of Applied Computing
**Field of study:** Applied Computing (GIS + IT specialisation)
**Dates:** Nov 2023 – Dec 2024
**Grade:** Distinction · Dean's List (Top 5%)
**Credential ID:** 170086552
**URL:** <https://www.lincoln.ac.nz/>

**Activities and societies:**

```
COMP693 Industry Placement at CORDE (NZ infrastructure & civil construction) — five-month on-site capstone, #1-most-prolific contributor on the team's React Native offline-first field-operations app, still in active production use by CORDE's field crews.
```

**Description:**

```
Master of Applied Computing with Distinction (top grade band) and Dean's List (top 5% of cohort) from Lincoln University, NZ. The programme bridged my geography/GIS foundation into modern software engineering — the same pivot I now make in every AI and cultural-tech project I ship.

What the programme covered:

• Studio model — enterprise software shipped to real industry clients using object-oriented design and agile workflows. The practice that prepared me to run solo CTO and Founding Engineer work.
• Advanced database systems — transaction processing, security, NoSQL, schema design and optimisation. The foundation behind every multi-tenant Postgres + Drizzle system I've shipped since.
• Applied neural networks — model calibration, data visualisation, real-world problem framing.
• Geographic Information Systems (GIS) + IT specialisation, building directly on my Geography Science Bachelor's.

NZQA-verifiable credential ID: 170086552.
```

## 2. Jiangsu Normal University

**Degree:** Level 7 - Bachelor of Geography Science
**Field of study:** Geography Science (GIS + Remote Sensing specialisation)
**Dates:** Sep 2012 – Jun 2016
**Grade:** Distinction
**URL:** <https://www.linkedin.com/school/jiangsu-normal-university/>

**Activities and societies:**

```
Field Practice in Geology · Regional Geography internships · spatial-data analysis projects on university-grade GIS facilities.
```

**Description:**

```
Bachelor of Geography Science with Distinction from Jiangsu Normal University, China. Specialised in Geographic Information Systems (GIS) and Remote Sensing — the spatial-data foundation that later carried me into software engineering, AI, and the GIS + IT specialisation of my Lincoln Master's.

Core coursework:

• GIS + Remote Sensing — spatial data analysis, cartography, working with university-grade GIS facilities and faculty.
• Computer Applications and Programming (Visual Basic) — top scores of 96 and 86 on practical software-build exams; my first formal programming credentials.
• Advanced Mathematics, Economic Geography, Urban Planning — first-principles reasoning across environmental, economic, and built-environment systems.

Why this matters for an AI engineer: spatial reasoning and bridging numerical models with real-world systems are the same muscles I apply to multi-agent AI architecture today.
```
**Media:** `Jiangsu Normal University`

## 3. Nanning No.2 High School

**Field of study:** Science (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology)

**Activities and societies:**

```
Student Council — leadership roles, inter-class academic competitions, and cross-disciplinary science-track collaborative projects across multiple years.

**Description:**

```
Founded in 1906, Nanning No.2 High School is one of Guangxi's flagship secondary schools and ranks among China's top 100 — a rigorous environment with nationally-recognised teachers and a long science-track tradition.

I studied the science stream (Mathematics, Physics, Chemistry, Biology) and graduated A+. This is where the first-principles analytical mindset that later anchored my Geographic Information Systems work, my software engineering, and now my AI-agent architecture practice was first formed.

Active in Student Council across multiple years — leadership, inter-class competitions, and collaborative projects that sharpened the working habits I still rely on every day as a solo CTO and Founding Engineer.
